options.messageDesc: Lust & Sloth Advantage check. Pass to gain a Keywordless E.G.O Gift.

971058.desc:
Something is breathing heavily. The source of it, its form strange, lay there.

It appears to be an Abnormality. The thing is struggling to even breathe—its every breath is laborious and irregular.

Is it... crying?
971058.name: Refracted Sleeping Bag of a Bygone Day

options.messageDesc: Run a Pride & Envy Advantage check. Pass to gain a Poise E.G.O Gift.

971059.desc:
The bloodsteeped petals are so crimson that one might almost mistake it to be bleeding.

Its stems, siphoning blood from somewhere to feed the petals, are twisted, dark, and red.

Vicious and sharp thorns riddle its skin.

But curiosity takes hold—perhaps breaking off a thorn might cause it to bleed?
971059.name: Refracted Four-hundred Roses

options.messageDesc: Lust & Envy Advantage check. Pass to gain a Bleed E.G.O Gift.

971060.desc:
This is an Abnormality of despair that has gone through a repeated cycle of faith and betrayal, leaving only the emotion that is expressed through black tears.

The hero, once a self-proclaimed knight and a protector of justice, is nowhere to be seen.

So many things that were once thought to be immutable changed, and her once-steadfast pride lay weathered and eroded.

Her eye socket, the endless wellspring of tears, is now twisted black; only the dark, burnt teardrop remains beneath it.
messages: I've failed to protect others, too.
971060.name: The Knight of Despair Containment Unit

options.messageDesc: Run a Gloom, Pride, & Envy Advantage check to gain a Sinking E.G.O Gift.

options.message: I've failed to protect others, too.
options.messageDesc: Run a Gloom, Pride, & Envy Advantage check to gain an E.G.O Gift.

971061.desc:
The Containment Unit's door was open.

Inside the unit was a girl, whispering, muttering to herself while curled up on the ground in gloom.

"There's good, and there's evil in this world."

"Where there's light, there's darkness. It can't entirely be one way or the other."

"I'm good, so they're evil."

"I am a chosen one."

"Am I unnecessary?"

"I hate... peace...?"

Anxiety. Compulsion. Obsession. Exhaustion.

Dark energy radiates around her like a materialization of those very words.
messages: We don't live in a binary world defined only by good and evil.
What if you were to bring an end to peace?
971061.name: The Queen of Hatred Containment Unit

options.message: We don't live in a binary world defined only by good and evil.
options.messageDesc: Run a Wrath, Pride, & Envy Advantage check to gain a Charge E.G.O Gift.

options.message: What if you were to bring an end to peace?
options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Charge E.G.O Gift

971062.desc:
There's a report on a Central Command Team Clerk's desk.

<DAY ■■, ■■ ■■ Research Service Postmortem Report>

The report was partially censored, but it seemed to be talking about a newly developed shield bullet of sorts.

'The Trauma Shield can protect its user from psychological damages from external sources for a ■■■■■■■ ■■■■ or ■■■■■■■■■.'

'If Shield bullet ■■■■■■ is needed, request the manager or a ■■■■■■■■ for assistance.'

Next to the heavily redacted report is a box labeled 'Trauma Shield sample & manual'.
971062.name: Lobotomy Corporation Central Command

options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Keywordless E.G.O Gift

971063.desc:
We're in a storage room at Lobotomy Corporation.

There's a broken machine—filled with what look like chunks of flesh.

"What... is that? Why's that just lying around in a storage room?"

"Faust explains that it is the remains of a previous iteration of a Sephirah, disposed upon replacement."

"... So it's a dead body?"

"Not exactly. Only its memories are reset; with replacement, the identical version of the Sephirah upon its initial production is reproduced."

"Almost a selfsame way in which we are rewound."

"Yeah, kinda like us, huh?"

Things got a bit awkward.

What can I, as the manager, say to lift the mood?
messages: I know we'll do fine. Like we always do.
We could fail. Like we always do.
971063.name: Lobotomy Corporation Storage

options.message: I know we'll do fine. Like we always do.

971064.desc:
"Ah, there...!"

I turn around to see someone precariously stumbling by the railings... before falling.

Falling... like a feather dropping from a height that is neither too high nor too low, like someone slowly sinking in water.

"WATCH THYSELF!!!"

One of the Sinners shouts vigorously to alert us to the danger.

We all sprinted toward the area where the person was falling, but it seemed that they were still falling by the time we got there, and we even had time to catch our breath.

Something about the ponderousness of their fall made me wonder if it would be as easy and light as grabbing a gently falling piece of paper to grab them.

"... Mass as a factor can hardly be ignored in this case. Despite the ponderous speed with which they fall, if we were to bring them to a sudden stop..."

"We'll get to watch every millisecond of their spine slowly turning to crumbles, and the subsequent, decidedly final agony, stretched to minutes." 

"Eww..."

We still have some time before they hit the ground. We have to find something to break their fall...
messages: Find something to break their fall.
Reach out our arms to grab the falling civilian.
971064.name: Daily Life

options.message: Find something to break their fall.
options.messageDesc: Run a Lust & Sloth Advantage check to gain a Tremor E.G.O Gift.

options.message: Reach out our arms to grab the falling civilian.

971065.desc:
"Halt! This area is off-limits to outsiders!"

A man wearing a tall top hat, presumably an employee of T Corp., stops us.

"Only those contracted by T Corp., or those with special authorization slips, may pass through here."

Realizing that we were just standing there dumbfounded and unsure as to what we must do, he points to the side and continues.

"Hmm, looks like you do have the right to pass through here. Perhaps you've misplaced your pass."

"You can request for a reissuance there, off to the side. Go try your luck there."
971065.name: T Corp. Collector

options.messageDesc: Lust & Sloth Advantage check. Pass to gain a Keywordless E.G.O Gift.

971066.desc:
We turn into a corner and come across a rather crudely drawn poster.

Written on the poster is a brief introduction next to a small note with directions.

'Running low on time, but burning full of frustration? Then come join the Yurodiviye.

Are you content with letting the inequity walk all over you?

Or would you rather live for its destruction?

Desire it, desire it hard!

Take this poster, let it be your train ticket.

At the ■■■ alley, face the ■■ road and walk at a speed of 120km per day. Alone.'
messages: Take the poster, and follow the directions on the note.
971066.name: Yurodiviye

options.message: Take the poster, and follow the directions on the note.

971067.desc:
A desperate voice echoes from afar.

"Join us. Let's tear down the inequities, together."

Another voice speaks.

"We're saving time from their poor, miserable deaths. This is a holy deed."

Another voice speaks excitedly.

"Join us, mate. Become one of the phantom thieves. Know them blokes, right? The gentleman thieves, the thieves who serve justice... Hm, or are we more like muggers of justice?"

... Maybe we can use this as an opportunity to trick them.
971067.name: The Time Ripper

options.messageDesc: Run a Lust & Sloth Advantage check. Pass to gain Cost.

971068.desc:
"Hey, you've come at just the right time. Listen, I need your advice."

We walked into a room with two Heishou glaring at each other, both of their buggy eyes bloodshot and clearly angry.

They explained to me that a Heishou of the You branch, in service of Xichun, happened to get into a kerfuffle with another Heishou.

"... And that's about the gist of it. Good thing they're both from my branches. A simple punishment wouldn't have been enough otherwise."

Like Xichun said, it seemed that they had decided to punish the Heishou of the You branch, who started the fight.

"How should I go about this?"
messages: Take the Heishou - You's talon—the Xueyan Blade.
Sentence the Heishou to a weeklong detention in binds.
971068.name: Punish the You branch Heishou.

options.message: Take the Heishou - You's talon—the Xueyan Blade.
options.messageDesc: Wrath & Gluttony Advantage check. Pass to gain a Burn E.G.O Gift.

971069.desc:
One after-school afternoon.

Xichun invited the Sinners to her office.

One of the Sinners notices a piece of red cloth on the ground, and inquires about it—but she only tilts her head curiously.

"That's not mine. That's... weird. I thought I cleared out all of the former Jia Mu's stuff..."

The red cloth was soaked in what appeared to be ominous, tarnished blood.
messages: Offer to take it with us if she has no need for it.
Tell her that we don't need it.
971069.name: Suspicious Red Cloth

options.message: Offer to take it with us if she has no need for it.
options.messageDesc: Pride & Gluttony Advantage check. Pass to gain a Slash E.G.O Gift.

options.message: Tell her that we don't need it.

971070.desc:
It's another day of classes for Jia Xichun, who's trying her best to prove her right to be the Family Hierarch.

After class...

... Jia Xichun came to visit the Sinners who taught today's classes.

"Guys, is it office hours? There were some things I didn't quite understand about today's class..."

The Sinners... or, rather, the teachers, turned to pay attention to Jia Xichun's questions.
971070.name: Office Hours for Jia Xichun

971073.desc:
The black market of District 8 Backstreets were full of interesting yet dangerous items.

Boluses that make you smarter at the cost of hair loss.

An Anamnaworm of someone who has tasted all kinds of wonderful cuisines.

But the one that stood out to us was...

"...? What's that doing there?"

"Dante, that thing's clearly been fenced. Maybe we can... "yoink" it, if ya know what I mean?"
messages: Admonish the merchant, claim that the gear was stolen from us

971080.desc:
In the midst of our battle against the nigh-endless Bloodbag horde...

<Hm...?>

... we came across an ominous object on the ground.

While it resembled the Bloodbags' weapons somewhat, there was enough to distinguish it from them.

"It's gotta be a powerful weapon, right?"

"It has got to be a rather dangerous weapon."

"Verily, 'tis an item most puzzling!"

Clearly, the Sinners are having a hard time coming to a consensus.

... Looks like it's up to me to decide whether we're picking that thing up or not.

971081.desc:
W Corp. Agents who turned into Bloodbags made for higher difficulty enemies than the other Bloodbags aboard the train.

Once they were defeated, we found a strange-looking gear from the aftermath of the battle.

I'd seen a W Corp. Identity with that gauntlet before.

"This gear is gunked up with gore; it'll be difficult to use it as-is."

Looks like we'll have to go through considerable trouble to bring it back to working order...

971083.desc:
This section seems to be rather crowded with passengers.

Among them, we notice a surprisingly calm and collected passenger.

"Come closer, won't you...?"

There was a bottle full of suspicious looking pills in his outstretched hands.

The passenger proceeds to explain that his present sanity is thanks to the pills in his possession.

"Woud you like to try some yourself?"
messages: Just take a quick look, then leave.

971084.desc:
"Lo! A lone warrior battles a horde of Bloodbags!"

The Sinners were looking at someone wearing a W Corp. uniform.

He seemed rather skilled, but the sheer number of Bloodbags were about to overwhelm him.

<We've got to help—>

But I am cut off by another horde of Bloodbags that poured into this cabin, surrounding us.

"Dammit... prepare for battle!"

"Y-yet what of the lone warrior?!"

"Well—"

Send a Sinner to assist the W Corp. Agent.

971085.desc:
Long eaves of the tile-roofing cast an impenetrable umbra. Beneath it stands a figure, submerged in the shadow, leaning against the wall.

They regard us in momentary silence before muttering in a low voice a question.

"Are you... prepared to yield your flesh, if it meant cleaving into your enemy's bones?"
"Take this blade, if you carry such determination."

Even at a glance, there's something about the sword in the figure's outstretched hand that gives off a disturbing air.
971085.name: Yield My Flesh

options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Cursed E.G.O Gift

971086.desc: Behind an old billboard that's seen better days stands a vending machine emblazoned with a half-faded T Corp. logo. The machine is tall, with dozens of thin clock hands ticking and turning down its cylindrical body; there's a promotional tagline written beneath it. "Stop borrowing time!" "Pre-pay with your disposable time, and buy time without delay when in a pinch!" There's a small slot next to the machine for users to insert their hands. Wait, underneath the slot—there are warnings written in nigh-illegibly small font. "Upon use of this device, with the proviso that the user makes a payment of a sum of temporal resource, the user will be imposed a temporary time shortage open parenthesis for the purposes of this agreement, the "temporary time shortage" mentioned will be predicated on the T Corp. Variable Time Scale; however, any sum of a day or greater will be measured based on the A Corp. Standard Time Scale close parenthesis; received time prepayment will automatically be deposited and amassed within the ancillary accumulation device within the machine and will be prepared for provision to the user upon circumstances in which they make a withdrawal request via undertaking a series of procedures or the fulfillment of refund conditions stipulated in the manual thereof, by summation of the user's total per-day hours and the extrapolated acceleration rate subsidies calculated by a distinct formula; the precise period, order, and limitations in reflecting these changes to the user's account balance is determined by the device's terms of service as described—" "V-very well. The full extent of this multiloquous writing needn't be recited in full." "Hang on, ain't this one of them things the Collectors wear on their arms?"
971086.name: T Corp. Time Prepayment Apparatus

options.messageDesc: Select to gain a Cursed E.G.O Gift

971087.desc: We see an old voice recorder with a faded label. The "Play" button is smooth and worn from use—as though someone pressed it hundreds of times.
971087.name: Audio Logs of That Day

971088.desc:
We come across two bizarre lifeforms standing on either side of a table set with an old roulette wheel.

"Care you test your luck? A prize awaits those who have fortune's favor."
"Lose. No prize. Leave if don't want."

The old roulette wheel they point at is inscribed with strange symbols.

We cannot make sense of the symbols, but something about them keeps drawing our gaze.

What should we do?
971088.name: Recurring Faith and Promise

971089.desc:
'Eunbong's Bar & Fryers!'
'Dial 555-0102-0386-2664! That's 555-0102-Zero-EUN-BONG!'

We followed the weird, catchy jingle and found ourselves standing before an oddly familiar machine.

"Hey, that's a claw machine. It's got... Huh, these are..."
<... Why are there Bongy plushies here?>
"Didn't that guy say something about how the one he gave us was an invaluable, one-of-a-kind treasure? That there's nothing else like it?"
"... Such promise carries very little weight in the Mirror Dungeon."
"Mm. It may be an idiosyncrasy of the experience that is yet to be identified."
"Guys, hold on. Look at that plush there—I just saw a golden twinkle through its seams! There's gotta be money in these things!

There are Bongy plushies of various design inside the claw machine.
Which one should we go for?

There's a note taped to the wall next to this unit—"Insight Work is effective." The Qliphoth Counter has already dropped to 10, but we don't see any Agents around. "I think, uh, Miss Hod or someone warned us about something like that in one of our past visits...?" "Mm. We were told that an increase in the Meltdown Gauge could trigger the arrival of Ordeals." "Wha? Then we'd better get crackin'! Hurry inside, you lot!" "Y-ya sure it's a good idea for us to go in there?" Would it be okay if we just left this here and moved on?

971093.desc:
The elevator door opens into a dark, unlit hallway.
We can smell the vivid and intense aroma of fresh, blossoming flowers that have grown over the walls and the floors of the hallway.

Lying lethargically atop the bed of flowers are employees, their eyes shut as though in drowsiness.
messages: This time, give up on them.

971096.desc:
The employee at the laboratory began in a level voice.

"Ah, you're right on time. The higher-ups ordered us to observe the subject's responses to types of Work other than Insight Work."
"Don't worry, it won't be very complicated. Just hold your breath, take this piece of meat, and hand it over to the bees.
"I'd really appreciate your help, if it isn't too much trouble."

I followed his gaze to the surveillance monitor of the containment unit, its camera pointed toward a massive bee with a torso that looked more like a beehive.

971097.desc:
There's someone crying inside the containment unit.
We peered inside to find a suspicious figure, wearing an outfit that stood out from those of the employees.
Noticing our presence, he suddenly approached us, beaming.

"I-I have to tune my instrument to perfect accuracy, but I don't have anyone to help me."
"Do you... like music? Help me, and I'll give you one of my instruments."
"H-how about it? Deal?"

971100.desc:
Perhaps the containment unit's security management has grown lax.
The Ardor Blossom Moth drifts languidly along the corridor, trailing a cluster of bright-red embers and flames in its wake.

Before long, the facility's announcement system erupts with warnings of a containment breach. Researchers come rushing down the hallway, scrambling to respond.

Should we step in and help the researchers suppress the Abnormality, or leave it to them and be on our way?
messages: Step in and help with the suppression.
Leave the suppression to the researchers.
971100.name: Ardor Blossom Moth Containment Breach

options.message: Step in and help with the suppression.
options.messageDesc: Wrath Advantage check. Pass to gain a Burn E.G.O Gift.

971101.desc:
A fluorescent, glowing hue of teal is dancing and rippling somewhere down the hallway.

An inexplicable impulse to walk toward the light takes hold of us.

Yet, seeing the researchers rush toward it as though drawn by an irresistible lure, it becomes clear that this is the doing of an Abnormality.

Should we force the researchers back and help suppress the Abnormality, or should we steer clear of whatever trap may lie ahead and move on?
messages: Step in and help with the suppression.
971101.name: Faelantern Containment Breach

options.message: Step in and help with the suppression.

971102.desc:
We can see the Shock Centipede beyond the glass of the containment unit.

"Ah, we're in the middle of studying how the Shock Centipede becomes effectively 'immortal' for a short while right before suppression."

"You see, our team's research paper focuses on discovering potential energy sources that allow for highly efficient charging gears."

"I trust you're all aware of the name of the E.G.O Gift extracted from this Abnormality?"

... What was it again?
971102.name: Shock Centipede

options.messageDesc: Select the right answer to gain a Charge E.G.O Gift
Select the right answer to gain a Charge E.G.O Gift

971103.desc:
"Let's see what it says... 'Entry Forbidden'...?"

We'd just passed the wall inscribed with messy, frantic letters when another door came into view.

The door was riddled with dents, as though something massive had repeatedly slammed into it with brutal force.

"At this point... think we could just force it open?"

"Nay, the current yet courses through it. It would be more prudent to open it by the rightful means and keep from inviting the consequences of crude handling."

A card reader panel is attached beside the battered door.

What should we do?
messages:
Open the door with the Operation Authorization Card.
<size=75%><color=#a16a3b>Choice unlocked with the E.G.O Gift "Operation Authorization Card"</color></size>

971104.desc:
Researchers of the LCE are running about busily, each carrying various items recovered from the House of Spiders in their hands.

Recognizing something from the pile, we stood around gawking at the item—until one of the researchers finally decided to talk to us.

"Oh, those are samples taken from the Corridor that looked like the Ring's gallery. We're done with the component analysis, so feel free to take them."

What should we do?

Grab something made of flesh and animal hide.

971105.desc:
A gentle, calming music playing from an unknown source tickles our ears.

Following the sound, we came upon an area containing multiple pieces of artwork.

We began taking a cautious look around, realizing that we'd probably just walked into one of the Ring's galleries... and came across a pedestal for an artwork—except it had no art on top of it.

It was small enough to lift with one hand, so it wouldn't be too much trouble to take it with us.

Should we?

options.messageDesc: Lust Advantage check. Pass to gain a Keywordless E.G.O Gift.

971106.desc:
We explore the hall filled with endless stretches of display cases. The ceiling is so high that we can't even see where it ends.

At first, I tried counting how many toy boxes we'd walked past, but soon lost count...

"What's this?"

... and came upon an articulated hand model, standing proud in its lonesome glory.

The fingers are curled into a fist, as if to grasp something between them—a knuckle duster.

Maybe we can put that to use if we can manage to pry it from the fist?
971106.name: Quiet Corridor of the Middle

Their duties completed, the Heishous are handing out the Mao Boluses they received from the Heishou-Xianren.

"And you, who are you? I haven't seen you around..."

"... Identify yourselves."

971109.desc:
We see a group of the Middle's thugs blocking the path ahead, all donning the same attire as the ones we saw at the Great Lake.

Going right through them would risk a battle.

Looking around, we find a lone boombox some distance from them.

Is that the source of the raucous music that plays in the background whenever these thugs show up?

Perhaps moving it somewhere else would bait the thugs away from the road with its music.
971109.name: Oddly Familiar Boombox

971110.desc:
"Um, excuse me..."

As we were slowly looking around the streets of S Corp., a youth, after fidgeting and hesitating for a moment, walked up to us and asked for help.

The youth explained that, wishing to better his own status, he took the Royal Examination—akin to a public employment policy in S Corp.—and was able to attain a high grade for himself.

And that was where his problems began; as far as he knew, people who passed the exam with flying colors like himself had their travel expenses paid for the homeward trip and were to be granted a guide. But there must've been a misunderstanding or something, because when he left the exam grounds, no one was there to escort him.

Confused by the unexpected turn of events, he wandered away from the exam grounds in search of his escort, lost his bearings, and found himself in these alleys.

He's asking if we could help him with directions... What should we do?
messages: "Sorry, we've never been here before, either."
"Let me ask my group if they can help."

971111.desc: Navigating our way through the streets of S Corp., we come across a young man hugging a blade, his gaze wandering blankly toward the sky. "... Ah." He seemed to have caught us staring—he turned his head toward us, gave us a once-over, then slumped back where he sat. "Pass on by, folks. I have no intent of bringing you harm." His voice was familiar... but I couldn't quite place my finger on it. "Search not for a revelation in the clouds drifting by, for they hold no answers." Kim blurted out of the blue as we were about to walk past the sword-hugging man. Oh, then that man must be... "... Do you know me?" "Must I know you to impart some unsolicited advice? A meddler in me simply cannot help himself when walking past a disadvantageous game of baduk." The man widened his eyes in brief surprise, then began in a contemplative voice. "You are a player of greater dan than I—that I can tell; so if you will, permit me to solicit some advice." "Shamed I am of my past plays, for they were dishonorable and deserving of ridicule; thus I had begun to wonder if I would be of better use should I become a blade for hire..." "Then, a man of incorruptible integrity, a just soul, sought me out and asked me that I serve as his blade. Yet, I worry that my filth may befoul his hands, too." "What say you? Would you say that I take this opportunity and make this play? Or..."

I catch a glimpse of a figure, staring off into the darkness of the unlit prison.

"Hah..."

Bamboo-hatted Kim lets out a sigh, but before he even fully exhales...

"Who goes there?!"

A sharp cry cuts through the stagnant air. Someone here must've noticed us.

What should we do?

Unsheathe our weapons and stand ready for battle.
Say that you are, and explain the situation.
